title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A 24-foot ice sculpture reading 'End ICE' was destroyed by vandals after the 'No Kings' rally in Boston Common.
The artist, Kat Dressler, designed the sculpture to maintain its message even if vandalized, as it would still end ICE.
Boston police were dispatched to the scene, but no report was filed; a nearby progressive group sign was also destroyed.

title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Antonio Merced, 29, was sentenced to 30 years for sexually exploiting three minors, including a 6-year-old girl.
Merced pleaded guilty to sexual exploitation and possession of child pornography in July 2025, as per federal prosecutors.
His federal sentence will run concurrently with a 25-30 year state sentence for related offenses involving minors.

title: 4 takeaways from Robert Kraft’s NFL meetings interview
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Robert Kraft expects a challenging 2026 season for the Patriots due to a tough schedule with extensive west coast travel.
Kraft supports adding an 18th regular-season NFL game to boost league growth and accommodate more international games.
The Patriots are opening the New Balance Athletic Center, a new team facility at Gillette Stadium, enhancing team operations.

title: Jayson Tatum earns his first Eastern Conference player of the week award of the season
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum wins Eastern Conference player of the week for the first time this season after returning from injury.
Tatum averaged 25.7 points, 9.7 rebounds, and 6.7 assists over three games against Thunder, Hawks, and Hornets.
Tatum became the youngest Celtic to score 14,000 points, joining eight others in franchise history.

title: Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown to return for the Celtics against the Hawks after missing two games due to Achilles tendinitis.
Derrick White set to play against the Hawks, recovering from a right knee contusion that benched him Sunday.
Jayson Tatum will not play against Atlanta due to Achilles repair management after recent return from injury.

title: Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Takeda to lay off nearly 250 employees in Cambridge, Massachusetts, as part of a restructuring plan.
The layoffs aim to achieve $1.25 billion in annual savings by 2028, approved by Takeda's board.
This move is part of Takeda's broader strategy to simplify operations and prepare for new product launches.

title: Lexington to cut a slew of teachers as town prepares to build $660M high school
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lexington Public Schools will cut about 65 full-time positions and issue non-renewal notices to 160 non-tenured educators.
Superintendent Julie Hackett cites declining enrollment and rising costs as reasons for the district's structural budget gap.
Lexington plans to construct a $660 million high school, while avoiding additional tax overrides to cover the budget gap.

title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, runs the Boston Marathon in honor of her mother's dream and to raise Alzheimer's awareness.
Wright has lost several family members to Alzheimer’s, and her mother was diagnosed with early-onset in her forties.
She runs to support Alzheimer's caregivers, advocate for research funding, and continue her mother's Boston Marathon dream.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Samantha Pelrine, a Plymouth police officer, was suspended after being charged with child rape along with her husband.
Pelrine and her husband Daniel Forand allegedly groomed and sexually abused a boy in their custody, per prosecutors.
The couple, who posted bail, are ordered to have no contact with the alleged victim and are due in court June 8.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police warn of possible tragedy after seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, involving mostly young riders.
The latest incident involved a 15-year-old losing control of an e-bike, colliding with a car; no injuries were reported.
Massachusetts law treats certain e-bikes like bicycles, requiring helmets and a minimum age of 16 for riders on public ways.
